Best Brow Kit I Have EVER Used!!! I have a thyroid condition which has really thinned out my once beautiful eyebrows, and this kit has given me my eyebrows back! I have blonde hair (brown naturally), and blue eyes, and my brows are light to medium brown. I buy the brow kit in dark blonde, the brown is much too dark. Once you buy the kit one time, you can buy the little "pot" of color and the the brow gel separately for a very reasonable price. I use this everyday, and the brow color lasts me about 5 months, the gel about 6 months...just make sure you don't use a lot of the gel, or you will have a wet look to your brows...just barely comb them with the mascara like brush to seal in the powder, and your brows will stay until you wash them off.

The tip I highly suggest is this, make sure you "load" the brush properly, and tap off the extra product, or you will have the powder on your cheeks and/or eyelids! I always start my makeup with my brows, so I rarely have a problem, and if I need to "clean up" a bit, just use a Q-Tip...they work great!

I always receive compliments on what great eyebrows I have....little do people know almost 1/2 of them are powder provided by Bare Escentuals!

Best value in $/oz for a true believer I bought this giant pack after trying the 16oz tea tree oil cleansing conditioner and did some crazy calculation on the total/average cost of different ways of purchasing this really expensive hair cleansing product from multiple sources.

Obviously, you can tell I'm quite convinced by this product, or else I wouldn't invest so much work/resources into getting it. Because these things take a while to use, I will be updating my review as I finish each flavor included in this set. I will not be using the Sweet Almond Mint, which is too oily for me and I'm sure you can find plenty of review on other package sizes.

* Overall Impression *

1. Great reduction in hair loss
2. Great increase in manageability
3. Quite expensive for regular use

A lot of people say "this product changed my life!" I don't want to exaggerate my excitement. I think to be very fair this product made me discover something I never experienced with other commercial shampoos - "near zero hair loss after wash".

I was a heavy shedder and I was used to losing a lot of hair everyday. With this product, I hardly lose any piece of hair at all during the day, with everything else being the same as my old-shampoo days. I have to say I don't know how it will compare to shampoo during hair wash with an equal comparison (i.e. same washing technique). WEN requires you to comb your hair during washing, which causes hair to shed when I wash my hair. I did not comb my hair while I was using shampoos so I didn't know if combing in wash would be the same. All else being the same, I lose about the same amount of hair during washing using shampoo or WEN. The no hair loss during the day is amazing though. I might have been allergic to the commercial shampoos and never knew it. My hair now feels significantly thicker when putting into a pony tail.

Another great discovery is that WEN makes my hair very smooth, light but not necessarily shiny. It dries faster after wash. It also feels easier to manage - my hair now stays in the right shape - this is especially true after the 24 hours. My hair would have started to feel lumpy and oily if I had used shampoo, but WEN keeps my scalp evenly moist a little longer. With these two improvements, I no longer use styling products or a blow dryer.

* Impression on the Flavors I Have Tried *

Tea Tree 5 of 5
Fig 5 of 5
Cucumber Aloe 3 of 5

Very short, I would recommend people with oily/combination skin type stay away from the Cucumber Aloe. Somehow it feels more water-based I had to use more to wash each time. At the same time, even though I make sure to wash it off completely, my scalp/hair started to feel oily sooner than when I use tea tree and fig versions. Both Tea Tree and Fig are great for oily/combination type. They make my hair feel clean and refreshed. Fig makes my hair feels extra smooth and it is the richest/densest of the three.

* How Long Does a 32oz Bottle Last? *

When the WEN TV infomercial says the 12oz starter version is a "30-day" supply, you need to understand that it is for people with very short, thin, or little hair. For someone like me with long, very thick Asian hair going to mid back, the 16oz tea tree bottle survived barely one month with washing at least every other day.

Two other things to keep in mind:
The 32oz pump holds more than a 16oz pump, but less than twice the amount.
Each flavor varies in its moisture strength. As mentioned earlier, I had to use more Cucumber Aloe to achieve the same effect I can do with Tea Tree and Fig.

I made a chart documenting my process of finishing up the 32oz Cucumber Aloe bottle. In total there were about 250 pumps, which lasted me 50 days with 30 washes, average 8 pumps per wash.

* How Much Should You Use For Each Wash? *

I definitely recommend strictly following the WEN instructions when you begin. That is, use the large amount as instructed by WEN, follow the steps to rinse, massage, comb, leave in, rinse off. This will help you see how the proper use can really change your hair as they've claimed. However, this is an expensive product so we all want to use it efficiently. Once you have formed the proper washing habit, start reducing the pump number little by little until you find your optimal dose. I found my optimal to be about 1/2 of the recommended amount for my hair length. Given the amount of hair I have, I'm sure many people can save more once you've started using it.

On average I use:
Tea Tree 16oz - 15 pumps per wash (haven't started the 32oz bottle yet)
Cucumber Aloe 32oz - 8-9 pumps per wash
Fig 32oz - 7 pumps per wash Wen is a winner! [[ASIN:B00183N9KQ WEN Complete Set of All Five Cleansing Conditioners - 32oz]]

Excellent for price, but somewhat misleading advertisement I just received this dermatoscope yesterday. For the price, it is excellent. It is a simple to use, contact dermatoscope that gives the user a bright, clear image of skin lesions. I like that it is LED since those seem to last forever, and it even has a lifetime guarantee on the light source. It is comfortable to hold and the carrying case fits well in my white coat.

My reason for not giving 5 stars is that the product description claims that it can be used with or without oil. I suppose this is technically true, as I use alcohol as my immersion fluid of choice. However, if this scope is used without any immersion fluid (as implied by "oil-free epiluminescence microscopy"), it is basically just a 10x magnifying glass. The purpose of dermoscopy is to help visualize structures below the stratum corneum. If the light is not polarized (which it is not in this dermatoscope), then an immersion fluid is necessary to reduce light reflection/refraction/scatter by tiny irregularities at the skin surface in order to see the deeper structures. If you want a true oil-free dermatoscope, you will need one with polarized light (note that these are generally much more expensive and do not necessarily produce a better image). I truthfully did not expect the claim "oil-free" to hold true, but purchased it anyway since I don't mind using immersion fluid and the price cannot be beat. Therefore, I'm not disappointed, but if you are considering purchasing this mostly due to the "oil-free" claim, I'd advise against it.

Basically, if you want a dermatoscope that will give you a great image at a great price, this is your product. If you do not want to use oil or another immersion fluid, make sure you purchase a dermatoscope that is polarized.

Update: October 2013 - After using my dermatoscope for three and a half years in residency and private practice dermatology, the contact plate (where the glass contacts the skin) broke. When I contacted the manufacturer they responded quickly and even offered to send me a free replacement. I continue to be pleased with this dermatoscope and the customer service that stands behind it.

Scent notes: Louisiana magnolia, golden Anjou pear, lotus flower, tuberose, star jasmine, pink cyclamen, vanilla-infused musk, sandalwood and blonde woods.

Can cupcakes & chocolate ever be a bad thing? I admit I was a little wary - I suffer from migraines so can't wear a lot of my favorites; including Angel by Thierry Mugler. Not so with FANTASY. This is a sweet scent, but the fruity & floral notes lend a touch of grown-up supplely.

Scent notes: Red lychee, golden quince, kiwi, cupcake accord, jasmine, white chocolate orchid, musk, woods, and orris root.

The soft floral notes seem creamer as they're enveloped in the crème brûlée & the vanilla & musk notes warm the fragrance up. IN CONTROL blurs the top, heart & base notes into one on me. Not Copal This is not Copal. I've traveled to the Yucatan and purchased both rock resin form of copal and a more convenient version in stick form. The hard resin form must have a secondary ignition source constantly present, like charcoal, in order to burn. The hand crafted stick version burns upon ignition just like any other stick incense.

This particular product is a stick incense labeled as Copal, but it is not. Perhaps it is a mixture that contains trace amounts of copal, if so, they are undetectable to my sense of smell. In other words, do not purchase this product if you are looking for a stick incense version of Copal that is traditionally burned throughout the Yucatan.

Business-wise, great seller (quick delivery, etc). Product is misleading and incorrect.

wrong way to advertise I bought this product thinking i was bying the same thing shown on the picture, The complete Evergo Portable Oxygen conentrator. WRONG!. what i bought was the extra battery. This is not the way to advertise this product. They should revise the way to put it for sale on Amazon. It's a good battery, but this is JUST for the battery As previous reviewers have noted, this is a placement for a BATTERY and ONLY a battery. Don't be fooled into thinking you've found a $4,000 portable oxygen concentrator for a fraction of the price.

Regarding the battery though, what can you say? It's a good battery. A single battery for an EverGo can you give up to 4 hours of use, but more than likely will give you closer to 2.5 or 3. It all depends on your LPM setting. Odds are you will want to. The battery itself should keep its full charge for over a year and then slightly decline it's max charge hold. Also, realistically you'll probably want at 2 for your unit to get that maximum time before you recharge.

The price is okay for the battery. That's pretty much what everyone is charging. If however you're looking to buy 2 of them shop around elsewhere. Most companies selling the EverGo or EverGo batteries will offer a deal if you buy 2 and bring the cost per battery down closer to $225 or even lower.

If you are looking for the full EverGo equipment however, be sure to speak your doctor first. Portable concentrators are not for everyone, so you should speak to a professional about it. Also, if you may want to look around for a company like [...] or others who will do Medicare or insurance reimbursement for the equipment. At $4,000 it's tough to come out of pocket for that kind of money, and if you can find a company that will bill your insurance it makes it a lot easier. Misleading advertizing This item is incorrectly advertized. It should say right up front that it is for the battery only and not wait until the very last line of the description. Prior to that last line, the description is for the concentrator - the reference might be that an extra battery comes with the unit.

Until this afternoon, I knew nothing about the prices of oxygen concentrators. When I saw this item, I thought maybe this portable unit was just quite small compared to the large floor standing models.

I know it's a "buyer beware" country, but I should think that Amazon would be more careful, if for no other reason than to protect their own reputation.

Item being reviewed: Respironics EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentrator - Extra Battery - A16285 03. Description: The Respironics EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentrator is an FAA approved portable oxygen concentrator developed by a trusted name in oxygen therapy equipment. Able to provide more oxygen in pulse settings per minute than any other portable oxygen concentrator, the EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentrator features a compact, lightweight design and long battery life, making it the ideal oxygen concentrator for those who like to travel. Buy your Respironics EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentrator today, and enjoy the freedom of travel without limitations! This is for ONLY the Extra Battery for the Respironics EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentrator. Respironics EverGo Portable Oxygen Concentator - Extra Battery OOPS! I ordered the item shown in the photo and it's not the "unit", it's just an "extra battery", which is of no value unless you have the unit, which we don't have. I had no idea "the unit" cost $4,000+ (I guess it's the unit that the "extra battery" is for).

This ad needs to be revised. Showing a photo of "the unit" and selling just the "extra battery" is not the way to go.

I Highly Recommend the AZ-1 Portable Automotive Hand Control System I have used this hand control system for approximately three months and I love it! Having no sensation in my feet due to multiple sclerosis, I became unable to feel the gas/break pedals and had to stop driving.
After reviewing all of the available systems on the internet, I chose the AZ-1 Portable. It sounded durable, easy, and safe.
The day I received my AZ-1, my dad came over and installed the system in my car. Then, we drove around my subdivision and a local parking lot so that I could "get the hang" of it.
Learning to use the system is fairly easy, BUT I would recommend practicing different scenarios. In a situation where you must STOP on a seconds notice, unless you have really practiced with the system, it would be very easy to push the gas instead of the break.
Saying this, after you feel very comfortable with the two controls, the system is perfect. I drive town and highway almost every day and the controls are like second nature to me.
I highly recommend the AZ-1.

Just love this so much! Truly blocks "BO" while allowing my pleasant natural scent through. Can't say enough about how much I love this product. Everyone should use it. Men and women.

The mineral salt deodorants work soooo much better than other natural "deodorants", which in my opinion are little more than essential oils that mask body odor, rather than actual odor prevention mechanisms. The mineral salts actually prevent odor from occurring by creating a non-viable environment for odor-causing bacteria. And it does so naturally and non-toxically. (Conventional deodorants are extremely toxic - especially the aluminum - do your research or just take my word for it.)

In fact, this product is so neutral, so unscented, and so mild that it allows some of my natural scent to come through - not the musty "BO" kind of smell... just my sweet-smelling, pleasant masculine scent... it's the best of both worlds, really. This is really refreshing compared to conventional deodorants which wipe out my pleasant scents entirely.

As for a comparison between this product and its most direct competitors - such as Thai Chrystal, which produces a very similar spray product - I have not found much of a difference. I do like that this packaging is more gender-neutral, compared with the Thai which looks more like a feminine product. I also believe that the Lafe's is slightly cheaper, ounce for ounce... plus it contains the Aloe, which I suppose must be good for something. :)

Oh! And I do like the spray MUCH better than the "sticks". The sticks are unwieldy, they are hard to moisten just the right amount to apply, and - worst of all - they shatter if you drop them, which is more common than it sounds since they are so awkward.

Anyway... I've been using this spray for years and I see no reason to stop!

smashing delivered as advertised Excellent for the price, more in packages than expected I'm amazed at how much hair comes in one package, and the texture and sheen is very close to human hair, and not too shiny like the nylon plasticky-looking stuff in cheap wigs. This looks believably like real hair. 4 packs is enough to make a really thick wig, and way more than enough to make a full head of long extensions.

I bought it to make myself a wig in "normal" human colors because I keep my hair dyed colors like blue or purple, but needed to look more professional sometimes. I wanted to try mixing reds to make it more natural and get it close to the red I wanted, so I bought almost every red offered.

"Dark copper red" and "red auburn" are exactly the same color, but made and packaged by different companies. Both are fairly dark and very red. One fell down in the package and became wavy, and the other had a kink where it was folded over inside the package, making both packages not smooth and hard to mix with the other colors.

"Burgundy" is slightly more natural than the swatch online looks (not like it grew out of someone's head that color, but more like salon hair dye and less like a punky non-human color), darker than the "dark copper red" and "red auburn," with a slightly violet undertone. For some reason, a tiny portion of the burgundy braid has a "yaki" hair texture, but only a small portion. The rest is silky straight. I don't know why the two different textures in one package. I will have to straighten the textured parts if I use it for anything.

"Dark auburn" is a very, very dark reddish-brown, with a tinge of black cherry color. Mine is smooth and straight.

"Strawberry blonde" is much more blonde than red and makes nice highlights in a red wig. Looks like a very natural blonde shade and the pack I got was really silky and smooth.

"Light auburn" is a slightly reddish dark blonde or light brown. When mixed with the strawberry blonde, it was my exact natural color. This color was also straight and smooth.

I also bought some non-human red shades to see how they looked and mix them with the other colors if I wanted them redder.

"Cherry red" is exactly what it sounds like: vivid cherry red. It is a slightly darker, cooler color than the regular "red." It's a beautiful color, very rich with a healthy hair shine.

"Red" is slightly brighter and leans a tiny bit more towards the orange end of the spectrum than the cherry red.

"Orange" is a really, really bright, traffic cone orange. It looks a bit more fake than the other colors, not as shiny, less like dyed healthy human hair and more like cheap doll hair.

I didn't mix the darker or non-human colors, only the two medium auburns and the two light colors, and together they make a very nice, natural red. So far I have mixed the colors and made the wefts on my sewing machine. The hair is easy to work with and hasn't tangled much. I have been using my straightening iron on the lowest setting on it and it hasn't melted at all, but does respond to the iron immediately. My only complaint, other than the two colors getting messed up in the packages, is that combing it even very gently with either a plastic comb or a metal wig brush causes some of the hairs to frizz up. I will update once I finish the wig and have worn it to see how it wears. Great for those who like to experiment with their hair Hair is cheap enough so you won't feel guilty about switching out colors. Great for those just learning about extensions or those who just want a few colorful highlights. It does get frizzy in a few weeks but is so cheap you can afford to replace it often. The texture is smooth and straight, silky but not fragile. The texture and shine is pretty realistic--maybe a bit on the shiny side--but since the hair is of a uniform colour, you'd have buy multiple colours and mix them in order to avoid the flat "bad dye job" look. The feel is a bit plasticy, but wigs aren't for touching anyway. The hair is resilient, and doesn't tangle easily or pick up static. The colour (dark auburn) was a fair bit darker than shown on the photo. I have a professionally-calibrated monitor for graphic design, so if the photo colours are off, it's not on my end. That said, I can understand if they did that on purpose: The hair itself is so dark that it would show up black on a lot of people's monitors if it they didn't lighten up the photo a bit. The quantity of hair is pretty good for the price, but I find I'd rather pay a little more for the bundle to include just a bit more hair--the amount per bundle is juuuuust a tad too thin to make a satisfying extension or ponytail, so you'd end up having to buy two and having a bunch leftover. Lame.

I received my order within four days. The hair had been folded up a little too tight in the packaging, so when I first unboxed it, it had a couple of kinks that fell out on their own during the wigmaking process.

The reason I'm giving it a four is because of the lies and deceit about this hair being suitable for curling. Granted, you should never take a styling iron to synthetic hair no matter how heat-resistant it claims to be, but I had that previously-mentioned "bunch leftover" so I gave it a shot on a low temperature. The hair came out melted and frizzy-but still straight. Why god why would you tell people that their hair can be heat-styled if heat styling will ruin their hair? For the wig itself, I used the standard steam-styling method, which worked just fine and didn't damage the hair.

Worst henna experience I've had I did a lot of research to find a new henna provider after my old one stopped carrying it. Henna Hut had a lot of positive comments and seemed to carry a quality product. I've been using henna/indigo on and off for years, and have always loved my results. Usually I used a kit which had pre-combined henna and indigo for the dark brown to dark auburn shade I prefer. Just mixed it up with hot water, allowed to sit for a few, applied, wrapped my hair in plastic and left on for 2 1/2 to 3 hours. It covered my small amount of gray with red highlights and my hair was soft and shiny.

When I bought separate indigo and henna packets from Henna Hut, I followed their instructions. I used very warm water, mixed the appropriate amounts of henna and indigo together, applied to my hair, left it on for about an hour and a half, and rinsed out. I must wash my hair lightly after using henna or my scalp gets irritated. This has never been an issue with other henna preparations.

Well, I got dark coloring from the indigo, but no red at all. As a result, none of my gray went away. The grays that usually turned into red highlights stayed gray. The rest of my hair was nearly black. I didn't mind the darker color, but no gray coverage was annoying. Then the entire texture of my hair changed. My hair is usually very fine and soft. Now it's rougher, drier, and easily tangles and breaks. Even my hairdresser noticed the difference. After doing some more research, I came to the conclusion that at least some of the problem is that henna apparently "activates" slower than indigo. I needed to do what I used to do, and leave the whole thing on for another hour or more, or I needed to mix the henna portion sooner and let it sit. Henna Hut does not tell you this. Also, I have no idea why the indigo seems to have damaged my hair. This has never happened before.

The power of positive thinking changed my pregnancy I used Hypnobabies Pregnancy Affirmations from the very early days of my pregnancy. This helped a lot to ease my fears of miscarriage (due to some medical reasons, I have a higher-than-usual chance of miscarriage). The affirmations CD helped me trust my body, and rejoyce in the miracle of pregnancy and learn to visualize and expect the best. I actually started to beliece that my body was my friend, not my enemy! The affirmations reduced my fear and anxiety, and helped me feel more confident and calm. There are great suggestions like: "Pregnancy is natural, normal, healthy and safe... My changing body is radiantly beautiful... I eat nourishing food every day for my baby... my baby is safe within me now... Growing my baby is empowering for me... with every day that goes by, I feel more confident about my pregnancy... all decisions for my pregnancy are mine to make... I am strong, confident, assured and assertive" and so on. This made an amazing difference in my pregnancy. Even a few months after my baby was born (yes, healthy, happy, full-term, with a fabulous natural Hypnobabies birth), I still use some of these affirmations about confidence, choice, having a voice, and loving my body and taking care of myself.

The other CDs are amazing too - I especially love the sleep cd for expectant moms... it puts me out right away every time I listen to it, which is such a blessing with a little one needing my attention throughouot the night... using my 'peaceful sleep now' cue, I can drift right off to sleep again. An amazing, life-altering gift for any expecting friends, or the best gift you could buy for yourself or your partner! One Star Waste of money

The leg attaches to the picture by press fit compression of the leg's two-prong fork feature to be around a sort of rubber grommet. If securely attached I find the leg does not come off when picking up the picture and placing it again on the table top in the same orientation. The leg must be removed and replaced 180 degrees on circular grommet/mount on back of picture if the picture is turned over for the falling sand effect. Bottom line, pick up picture, remove leg, turn over as many times as you want, when satisfied, reattach leg and set picture on table top.

Glass Face - Face is glass, not plastic. Perfect. Plastic is more susceptiple to scratching in my experience and opinion.

Great Scene - Picture has a perfect photograph for complementing the sand (OK this might be apparent in the Amazon photos). What appeals to me is that there is the photograph where other sand pictures that have no photo leaves the viewer seeing what is behind the sand when the sand settles. I find seeing whatever happenes to be behind the picutre less appealing. Also, in this (Golden Sun) version, the sand includes yellow and orange sand (small amounts of both) that complement the photograph and make the sand scene appear like the tops of hills/mountains still receive sunlight.

Great Frame - Solid frame with small grain/fatigue surface features, also painted a shiny gold coler (thing shiny dark gold gift bow). It might have a clear coat of some kind, but I am not sure. Tight, quality joints and surfaces at corners with no mis-alignment, mismatched thickness problems. I am nearly certain it is wood, but I cannot be absolutely certain.

Good Dimensions - Dimensions are big enough to accomodate more than one "hill/mountain". My experience with smaller sand pictures tend to create only one or none or more very small ones.

Air/Water Adjustment - Parts includes a blunt syringe and instructions for adding air or removing air contained in the picture. This is done by inserting the syringe through a normally blocked, one-way gasketed hole feature (about 1/8 in. diamteter) in the side of the frame. I have never seen this feature for users in water-filled sand pictures by other manufacturers. I will confess to not having tried adding/removing air because I don't want to alter the amount that seems to be working just fine as received from the manufacturer. Instructions for doing so are provided and I found sufficiently clear.

Tilt Angle Potentially Problem - The only thing I would have changed if I could was to shorten the slightly flexible plastic leg so the picture doesn't tip back , it appears, by only 10 degrees or so from vertical where it is easily tipped forward if bumped. If it falls forward, it could break, particularly if the glass strikes an object when falling. I would have suggested, perhaps, 20 degrees tip for a little more stability.

Slightly Flexible Leg Feature Good - I suspect the leg is slightly flexible, rather than rigid, so that, if the picture is bumped from the front or set down on the table top surface a little more "vigorously" that the leg will flex a little rather than, otherwise, possibly resulting cracking or breakage of the leg or picture. Great idea Rainbow Vision.

I know I have written a lot. I hope it helps.

I have metatarsalgia from Morton's toe, so I need arch support to keep my feet from rolling inward and putting pressure on that second metatarsal bone. Additionally, my toes are very long, so I have to wear shoes 1/2-to-1 size bigger than I'm measured to, which means any built-in arch support is too far forward for my foot. As such, I have to buy inserts smaller than the shoes I wear, which means they tend to slide around.

I have had good luck with [[ASIN:B0033PSSJO Dr. Scholl's FOR HER Hidden Arch Supports, 1-pair Packages (Pack of 3)]], but wanted something that I could quickly wear with any shoe (as setting those sticky, in-shoe inserts takes a bit of time to get it positioned just right), as well as wear barefoot or with my slippers around the house.

Based on the rave reviews here, and that the image appeared to be of a product that has a wedge-shape and would push my insoles up and keep my feet from rotating inward, I ordered a pair of these. Unfortunately, when they arrived, I was sorely disappointed. Unlike the other inserts I have had luck with, these are a flat piece of gell-plastic. Yes, they have good adhesive and generally stay in place on my foot (after a few tries to get them in just the right place, which takes off a lot of dead skin each time I have to peel it off to try again, which makes them less sticky until I wash and let them dry), but they don't _support_. The fact that they are something extra on my arch offers the tiniest bit of support, but it is not nearly enough to keep my feet from rolling, and also wouldn't be enough for someone with fallen arches. I also found them to be uncomfortable to walk around in for more than 10 minutes, because they provide this lump in my shoes, as my feet start to roll over onto them.

I would try the extra thick version of this product, but since I know that they are just a flat piece of plastic, I know they still would not offer proper support and would probably provide even bigger lumps in my shoes. These should be a wedge shape, not a flat shape.

So, now that I've wasted $16, I'm going to go spend just as much to buy three pairs of the other inserts and just load up all of my shoes so it won't be a problem when I need to quickly slip on some shoes and go. And, so much for going barefoot or having support in my slippers. Arch support They stick well. Follow directions on box.They provide a good bit of arch support. The texture of the cleanser is like Elmer's glue, and you have to REALLY rinse it off, but if you do so, your skin will be like the fountain of youth.
Use this in confidence for a variety of issues, and you'll be glad that you did!

I have used a blade for over 10 years, and that was after many years of electric shaving (Remington/Braun). I have been using a Fusion for a while, but the price of blades seems high, they last no more than a couple weeks each and I'm always getting nicked around the chin area. (- what a life-saver those Styptic pencils are!). Then there's the foam and the mess. I decided to go back to electric shaving if I could find a cheaper alternative that gave me a reasonably good shave.
The Amazon reviews indicated this series of shavers fit my requirements. I chose the slightly cheaper ES3481K which does not have a trimmer or a carry case (despite what the product description says), but it does have the usual see-thru snap-on, foil cover.
My first impressions were - looks like an electric shaver; easy to access the cutter; once you work out how to open the battery compartment cover, that seems well-sealed against moisture; it's quite noisy with two new AA batteries, but no more-so than any other electric shaver; it's comfortable to hold and about the size you'd expect for a single blade shaver, but not particularly compact for travel - unless you compare it with expensive models that are often very large.
When using it for the first time (a dry shave) at lunchtime after a Fusion shave only 5 hours before, the shave was smooth, simple and fast. No nicks even where I got nicked this morning and the result is actually a really good close shave, especially around the neck area. I have found that even the Fusion cannot remove all the stubble on my neck without creating soreness, yet without much effort, this shaver has done an arguably better job. Not perfect, but who's going to know except my wife and she would like me to grow a beard!!
Overall, this shaver seems to meet the good reviews it has received on Amazon. I'm happy and who can complain at the price? The manual recommends a new foil every year and a cutter every two years - that's cheaper than the blades over the same time period, if it's true. As a reviewer said, it's almost worth buying more; for home, work, the car, etc. You can always have a close shave, because you can then shave several times a day.

I will update this review when I've tried the wet shave and after a few weeks of use.

Update #1: I've tried the shaver for both dry and wet shaves. Shaving was done in the morning both times.

For the dry shave I showered first, dried off and applied AFTA pre-electric shave lotion at the sink. The shave was good, the neck and jaw-line stubble took some extra time as always. Generally happy with the result.

The wet shave was in the shower, at the end of the usual shower routine. I used my body wash lotion - it has olive oil and tea tree oil in it, so provided a good shaving medium and lubrication. I rinsed off the foil a couple times in the shower spray during the shave. I found the shave more comfortable to do than the dry one and it seemed to achieve a much closer shave, even though, again, the neck and jawline took extra time to do thoroughly.
After each shave, I applied some liquid soap to the upright foil and ran the shaver until foam appeared on the foil surface, per Panasonic's instructions. Then rinsed the shaver under running water. This really keeps the cutting system clean and presumably sharp. Much better than brushing and blowing hairs all over the sink that I had to do with my previous dry-only electric shavers.

I really noticed the difference between the wet and dry shaves the following morning. The dry shave resulted in a lot of stubble the next day. The wet shave was significantly less stubble.

CONCLUSION: The wet shave gives the best result and is less irritating to the skin, due to the lubrication from the lotion. This little shaver really does a great job in the shower.

Update #2: Used everyday and I'm still on the same Duracell batteries I fitted when I bought the shaver! More than the 90 minutes battery life in the specs.

Update #3: May 7 2010 - Finally decided to change out the batteries after about 3 months. Noticeable increase in speed of the shaver and it seemed to have more power to slice through those whiskers. Maybe better to swap them out sooner rather than later, but the shaver doesn't really sound much different as time goes on.

I have settled on a dry shave each day using Afta and a wet rinse as described above. Not having a usable mirror in the shower made getting a complete shave tricky. The result is not as good but works well enough.

After 1 year of using the product, great traveling shaver First of all here are some points to put into consideration before buying. This shaver is for...
1. Someone who is looking for a TRAVEL shaver that is small and convenient to carry. It comes with a neat carrying case, and only requires two AA batteries that can be found anywhere. One set of batteries can approx. last up to 20 days/shaves-ish. It will still go on, but I can feel the motor running at lower speed.
2. Someone who prefers WET shaving experience whenever possible. (Wet shaving with this product is great. Dry, not so much.)
3. Someone who needs to shave everyday or simply want that clean shaven look everyday. ( It's still a foil electric shaver, it's fast, efficient, everyday.)
4. Someone who needs his travel shaver to have a trimmer. (Frequent long trip? just like me.)
5. Someone who doesn't want to spend 15 minutes cleaning the shaver. Who wants hassle-free and easy cleaning shaver.

This shaver however isn't for someone who is looking for a blade replacement shaver. I say, wake up, electric shaver will never give you that baby "skin" shave like you get from a proper blade shaving method. However, for $15 this shaver can deliver a very decent shave, very close, but definitely not alike. It also isn't quite for someone who plan to use it 7/365 days. You will probably be better off buying other wet/dry panasonic shaver that is rechargable, and have more than one moving blade, linear-super fast motor and on.

It's been a year and I still use it on every one of my trip. no problem. Impeccable reliability there, and well made.

--------------------

If you decide to buy it, here are my tips and tricks for you. It's based on my experience with it so perhaps it will help you get a better shave with this shaver.
1. YES, it will take up to 2-3 weeks if you are coming from blade for your skin to adjust. This is normal and all you need is patience to get through it.
2. It's miles apart better wet shaver than dry. DO NOT shave dry unless absolute necessary.
3. With that said, all the techniques in wet shaving apply. You should shave in the shower, or right after you come out of it. Your skin should be clean prior to shaving. One trick is that this shaver works better if your shaving gel/foam/cream is "light" or "not dense". Lather up too much or a very dense cream can make the foil/blade hot during operation. (Thick cream will cause more friction and blade moves pretty fast.) Last, don't forget a splash of cold water to seal in and a good aftershave.
4.DO NOT press the shaver, or foil against your skin. You will only get irritation out of it. The trick is to shave everyday, go against the grain, let it GLIDE SLOWLY on your skin, rinse it whenever you feel there is too much foam in the shaver, use trimmer to take out long stubborn hair like those on the neck, and under the chin. DONE.

I tried everything, Odaban WORKS, it cured me and saved my social life and self-esteem. It's worth every penny!!! My head (scalp, forehead and face) sweat A LOT. Odaban has saved my life!!!! After a couple days of applying, I immediately noticed a difference.

**********PRICE / VALUE************

The price is pretty steep, all things considered, but it's so worth it. I've tried EVERYTHING else. Even applying antiperspirant deodorant to my forehead. When you've tried everything else and nothing works, finding something that does makes it's price pretty inconsequential..... It is WELL worth it. Buy it in three packs, even if it's your first purchase. I guarantee you will be buying more, and it takes a while for some venders to get it in stock. It's much more popular in Western Europe than it is in the states (I think its even made there) so it can take a while for vendors to get it in stock, even on Amazon. So, save the trouble and save a few bucks and days, get the three pack.

**********GUARANTEED APPLICATION METHOD***********

I apply twice a day until under control, then once every other day or as needed. Once in the morning, after a shower, and once at night, right before bed. This application method works best for me to completely cure my excessive "head" sweat, use the same method to whatever area you need help with:

1. Apply right after getting out of the shower, after standing in front of a fan for a few minutes to make sure you're application area is completely dry. (this is important)
2. Spray liberally on my scalp, "Dabbing" my head to make sure it reaches my scalp.
3. 2-3 "swiping" sprays across my forehead, then one on each check.
4. Make sure all parts of face have odaban applied by "dabbing" out from the application areas.
5. Stand in front of fan for 5-10 minutes minutes, or until Odaban is completely dry (Also VERY important).

Waiting until the area is dry before applying, and letting it try after application, is very important. It makes all the difference in whether you will have successful results or not. Trust me! Stick with a variation of my method an you will have AWESOME results.

*********SIDE EFFECTS***********

Odaban will sting if it gets in your eyes. It's not the end of the world, you just know its there. Also, at first when applying twice a day, every day, I sometimes get a little irritation on my skin, or a burning/stinging sensation. I just deal with it, as it only lasts until I can start phasing down to only having to apply every other day.It's worth it!!!

I've heard of some poeple having "excruciating" stinging / burning....... Honestly, I think they are just wimps that probably complain about everything. For me the embarrassment off be soaked in sweat around a girl is way worse than a little stinging. Dying alone would suck.... A little stinging... Not so much!!!!!

Good luck, I hope this has been helpful. Please give me a vote if it has and feel free to ask questions!!!!!

Poorly Designed Heads Fill Up Fast The biggest problem with this design is that if you have a heavy/fast growing beard is that you may have to stop and clean it mid-shave. There just isn't any room for cut whiskers in the tiny heads and where a heavy 1-day growth is a maybe a 2-day growth is way more than it can hold. I think that may be why some people complain about the closeness and/or speed of the shave. It works really great until it loads up with fur and there is no room left for more. Note to Norelco: Increase the cut whisker capacity in next year's model. Almost there... First the good news...

Now the not so good news...

I bought it as a gold box special. If you pay full price you may need a second job
for the privilege of shaving! It feels mucho cheesey when compared with the older
razors. The design is attractive and flashy but it is over designed to the point
that it is actually less convenient to use. My example would be that you must open
each head individually to clean it and I wonder how many times this can be done before one of the heads breaks off.

My advice is to keep and eye out for a deal or wait until your old reliable shaver
fails and then bite the bullet. I am use to Norelco gouging me on the price of the
heads but now they are gouging on the price of the razor as well. When buying remember it is the type of head the razor uses that matters. The flashy crap just gives them an excuse to further increase their profit margin. Looks cool, but quickly overwhelmed. The Goldbox offer (half price) suckered me into buying this model, despite recently having happily switched away from Norelco to the Braun 360 Complete. One of the advantages of the Braun was the much better handling of longer whiskers as the occasional 1-week-slump generates. Hoping that the hype of the new shaving heads resolved this issue, I eagerly tried out the Arcitec. It's still completely overwhelmed and no competition for the Braun at all. For the daily shave, the Arcitec 1059X is quite able to provide a reasonable shave.

The design may be the main reason to get this model as the shape and the cool display are likely to grace every bathroom. It's modernistic, yet understated. Yes, this is one of the best looking shavers on the market! The shaving head is fairly flexible and adapts to contours well, As with any dry shaver I tried so far, it won't match a wet blade, but it's not bad either. In fact, with restricting the whiskers to a one or two day length, the results are fairly comparable to the Braun 360 which so far yielded the best results for me. Then again, past that point, the Braun is a clear winner loosing only to a wet blade (again).

Handling of the Arcitec is reasonable and style concerns certainly overrode function, but still to a subtle degree that's acceptable albeit not perfect. Battery life is excellent and the charging stand has actual suction cups to make it easier to keep the base planted on the counter top. There is also a travel case included which doubles as a charging cradle. (The power supply plugs into the case and contacts allow the razor to be charged when plugged in.)

Overall mostly a cool design and quite functional for the daily chore. However, if you're less diligent with your shaving schedule it's hopelessly overwhelmed with the longer (and thicker) whiskers. The lift'n'cut feature is standard for Norelco and reasonably effective. However, it also irritates the skin a bit more. Great hair cutter, easy to use. My wife is a professional hair stylist. She cuts my hair every 2-3 weeks and relies heavily on using electric shears to do so. This unit works very well. It can work plugged in, or with battery, which should give you about 40 minutes of time per charge.

The shears are sharp and do a great job of cutting through my thick hair. The attachments are also very nice. Unlike other units that have 6 or 7 different guards depending on what you want to do. It has a fixed length one, a short variable one, and a long variable one. For my hair she used the short variable one. The settings go from 1/16 of a inch up to about 3/4 inch, and the long variable one goes up from there.

Having the variable length guards is great as it reduces the number of guards you need and allows you to change lengths on the fly.

Using the shears without the guard is also good for trimming to the skinline, edging and shaping.

The only complaint my wife had is that she wishes it were a little more powerful on battery, but wasn't really lacking in power, it is just that she usually uses professional shears. This unit is actually as powerful or more powerful than most shears you buy at walmart or the like.

Overall I'm pleased with it. It cuts through hair quickly and easily making it easy for quick touch up trims. It's also fairly quiet, much quieter than my electric shaver so it doesn't affect my hearing when trimming sideburns and hair that's grown longer than my ears. It will also operate in either corded or cordless mode, handy when the battery dies.

Only a few negatives from my view:
-The charging LED in the handle does not shut off when the unit is charged. It is lit the entire time it's plugged in. Little annoying considering almost very rechargeable device will let you know when it has completed charging.
- It does not charge at the same time you are using it while plugged in. Not really a big deal, just something to be aware of.
- Only the head portion can be rinsed; the base / battery can not get wet. Again, not really a big deal.
- Hair trimmings get stuck in the lubricant grease inside the head. Not a lot, only a few very small pieces, but if you're a perfectionist it can be a little aggravating.

I'm expecting this will also work well to trim out the tufts of overgrown toe fur that my cat gets. I can, and do, trim with scissors periodically but it takes a bit of time due to being careful. 15 months later and I'm still using this. Does a fantastic job of keeping the hair around my ears trimmed just enough so that it isn't touching or rubbing. Very quick and quiet. It still irks me a little bit that it doesn't tell you when charging is complete. The other nitpicks I've gotten over.

Nice improvement This is a nice improvement to the Sonicare line of brushes. It appears to be their response to the Oral-B Floss Action line of brushes that have a "paddle" to help get between teeth. This accomplishes that goal with an plastic insert in the middle that helps direct the toothpaste between your teeth.

I use both the Sonicare (Healthy White) and the Oral B 5000. Both have their pluses and minuses, so switching between the two gives me the best of both worlds.

The Oral-B arrangement of paddles does a better job in getting at the gum line without discomfort. The Sonicare Hydroclean's bristles are stiffer and I find that they irritate my gums when used several times in a row.

But, the Sonicare does a better job in cleaning stains between teeth. I'm a tea drinker and getting the stains out between my front teeth has always been a chore. The Hydroclean works very well - noticeable improvement over the Sonicare ProResults and the Oral-B.

These only come in the standard size. The ProResults has a smaller version that is great for getting at the tight spots. But, I assume that a smaller version of this would not function well since the plastic insert would have to be very small.

If you're a ProResults user these are well worth trying out. If you have tender gums you may want to alternate between the HydroClean and the ProResults (or, as I do, with the Oral-B. The Oral-B also makes a super soft brush if you have very sensitive gums. Hard to find, but works very well).

Keep in mind that nothing takes the place of flossing. No matter what you use, you still need to break out the floss to keep your teeth healthy! Firmer Than Other Heads Compared to my regular replacement heads, these heads feel much firmer and more stimulating to the gum. They also snap more firmly to my brush than do my regular heads and run more quietly.

If I had to choose between the two, I'd choose my regular head first. Because, however, I've already bought these and they may have some beneficial effects the regular heads lack, I've chosen to alternate the heads. I use one head to clean my teeth with baking soda, then floss, then use the other head to brush with toothpaste.

I've now been using this head for two months. It only took a couple of days to get used to the stiffness and I wouldn't even notice it if I wasn't alternating the heads. I see no evidence, however, that it cleans any better. These are what I would consider to be the best brush heads for the Sonicare toothbrush that I've used ... OTC Progesterone Doesn't Do It For Me For the past 10 years I have tried one over-the-counter progesterone cream after another, including ALL of the brands recommended in Dr. Lee's book. Unfortunately, none of them worked very well for me. After 10 years of going from one brand to the next, I finally gave up, got my hormones tested, and had my doctor prescribe a compounded bioidentical formula for me.

The problem with over-the-counter progesterone is that you really don't have any precise way of knowing how much progesterone you're really getting, regardless of what the label says.

I thought I was taking 40 mg. of Natra-Gest a day. And yet, when my progesterone levels were actually checked, the pharmacist said the amount of progesterone floating around in me was almost non-existent. Go figure. This makes me wonder if there really is as much progesterone in over the counter creams as specified on the label.

Now I'm taking 40 mgs. of compounded bioidentical progesterone cream. At least I know how much progesterone I am really taking in.

ZRT Laboratories has simple saliva testing which you can order online. You spit into a tube, send the saliva back to them, and within 2 weeks, they send back your results with suggestions. They even give you an easy-to-understand summary about what they found. You can then take those results to your doctor and arrange to get a compounded progesterone made specifically for you.

Because of the many problems I experienced with over-the-counter progesterone creams over the past 10 years, I'll never buy them again. They just don't work for me.

I guess each woman's body is different. My doctor did tell me, however, that compounded progesterone cream and over-the-counter progesterone cream cannot even be compared. According to her, they are like "night and day." Those were her exact words.

If you find that you are not receiving satisfactory benefits from any over-the-counter progesterone cream, it may be time to think about trying a compounded progesterone, made specifically for you. These one-size-fits-all over-the-counter progesterone creams are not able to help all women, especially women who are in their late forties and are in the perimenopause stage of life.

I used to buy Vikki Lamotta Wonderlift YEARS and YEARS ago in New York, and then stopped using it to try a lot of other brands. Hers was the original, and it still works great. HOWEVER, you DO need to play around with it for a while to get it to look natural and so that your face isn't all powdery looking. I use a very light moisturizing lotion on my face FIRST and let it completely absorb before I apply the Vikki Lamota Wonderlift, and then I let the Wonderlift DRY FOR HALF AN HOUR AT LEAST until it is fully firmed up, after which a slight white chalky look is on my skin... THEN, I get my hands damp under the faucet (shake off all the excess water) and rub MORE of the moisturizing lotion into my hands and mix it with the little bit of water in the palms of my hands and then lightly PAT my hands all over my face where it appears white or chalky looking. I only use a tiny bit of make-up as a TOUCH UP ONLY on places that have blotches or redness, because you CANNOT rub makeup over your face once the Wonderlift has dried or it will simply ball up and take the Wonderlift OFF! It takes some practice, but if you are dedicated to looking YEARS YOUNGER (as I am!!) then this will do the trick! Not a miracle but does help Miracles I don't expect, so it does do some temp firming an tightening on small lines. It goes on nicely an feels pretty good too. Make up was easily applied over top. Stayed nice to 4-6 hours, which is good since I have very oily face.
Have to be careful to not use to much or it leaves white areas an needs to be blended or feathered at edges so it doesn't show.

Effective if applied properly I tried this product about 6 years ago. I was impressed with the results, but I had to get the hang of applying it properly.

The rule is "less is more". Applied with a light touch to the problem lines, and allowed to dry completely, you have a chance to remove any flakiness where too much got on. Use a slightly dampened QTip. Once you have "tidied up", you can then apply your foundation.

Another tip is to do the various line areas at different times. This takes bit longer, but it allows you to keep you face still while each section dries. Particularly where those pesky around the lip lines are - you need to maintain that area as still as you can with a normal expression, until the serum has dried.

I found that whenever I used it, I got a lot of compliments. I found it would last 4 to 5 hours. I think it's a super product if you have a photo shoot, or a special date, or some occasion where you really want to look great.

Good replacement for the extinct Bullfrog Surfer Formula I was a long-time user of Bullfrog's Surfer Formula. Bullfrog discontinued it, and the replacement didn't come close in ease of use and effectiveness. I did a search, hoping to find a hidden BSF supply. None to be found, but I saw a reference to Doc Martin's Nextgen. Hmm.

I decided to buy it. The product arrived. My first reaction was a little skeptical: 2 ounces isn't much!

I gave it a try the next day on a hot, sunny day. A little bit goes a LONG way, so 2 ounces should last a while. It is very easy to apply, and even has a pleasant scent. I was out in the sun 5 hours with one application, and didn't have any burn. It didn't run until I got a surprise face bath, and then I had some run-off in my eyes. But I haven't had any eye run-off otherwise, even on long, hot, sweaty, muggy days.

Doc Martin's Nextgen is a good substitute for the late Bullfrog Surfer Formula: it's easy to apply, it works for hours and hours, it stays put, but it's easy to remove when you want to remove it. I expect to be a repeat customer.

I'd first like to say, I love Brandon Carter. I've learned a lot from his YouTube videos, and when I saw him promoting this "fat-burner," I figured I'd give it a try to see if it would help me get somewhere near his shape. However, it seemed a little sketchy, the TeaRexx website doesn't work and after doing some research, I noticed the bottle is packaged in Forest Hills, NY which is where Brandon Carter's modelmayhem profile says he resides. Coincidence? If he is the one selling this or is getting endorsed to do so, it would've been nice if he admitted it. I mean his YT vids are free and nothing wrong with a man making some bucks, especially since he's given me a goal and tools to help me, but full disclosure is all I ask.

Anyway, basically this has green tea extract, Vitamin C, Vitamin B3, B5, B6, B12, panax ginseng, and L-taurine.
Let me also say I'm a doctor, and multi-vitamins have never been proven to provide any health benefit.
So does the pill work? I get NO EFFECT from the pill, it gives me no extra energy. It gives me no jitteriness, nada, nada.
I think you're better off drinking your own-made green tea. And there's lots of B vitamins in meat and whole grains.
The B6 in one capsule is 40mg or 2000% your daily rec, and B12 is 8333%, and the bottle recommends 3 pills a day!
The max recommended limit of B6 is 100mg/day from supplements, so 3 pills would be 120mg. I don't think this pill is dangerous, and maybe some people will feel more energy, but you could get B vitamins and green tea naturally for much cheaper than 50¢ a pill.

Hope this helps you guys, I think the other reviews written here are shills.
Lastly, still love you Brandon if you're reading this, and so I'm ok with the $40 donation for your vids.

It cleans very well, has a very light clean scent and doesn't seem to bother my sensitive skin the way many liquid hand soaps do. Normally I can't stand soap fragrances (like most men) but this scent is a mix of "traditional soap smell" and something very light and refreshing. (I don't care for their other scents of soap - just the pale green gardener's soap.)

It also helps soothe and moisturize your hands without the greasy feeling of traditional moisturizers or hand creams.
While it isn't really strong enough to remove grease, oil-based paints and the like, I use it after having used something harsher as it leaves my hands feeling soothed.

A modern classic in the fundamentals of card magic - but get the 2013 edition! A NEW VERSION

I recently picked up the new edition of Oz Pearlman's Born to Perform Card Magic DVD, which was released in 2013. I was curious how it compared with the original DVD that was released in 2003, and in this review I'll summarize what the differences are, and what the contents of the new edition include.

I bought my DVD of the 2013 version directly from the creator, Penguin Magic, but it's also available elsewhere online including at some retailers and sometimes on Amazon. The nice thing is that in addition to sending you the DVD of the new edition, Penguin Magic also gives you access to a digital download of both the 2003 version and the 2013 version, so you get the best of both worlds. Both videos are videos in *.mp4 format, with the 2003 video being 753 MB in size, and the 2013 video being 1.43 GB in size.

LENGTH

The new edition is billed as an updated/expanded version, and that's clearly evident when you compare the length of the two videos:
2003 version: 1 hour 44 minutes
2013 version: 3 hours 48 minutes

DIFFERENCES

So what's the difference exactly? Well for starters, Oz himself is now 10 years older, and has a lot more professional experience under his belt. He has a new look and even a new smile. Everything has been re-filmed and the explanations have been re-done from scratch. Most of the content covers the same material, but not exactly - I'll mention what exactly those differences are in the next section.

CONTENT ON THE ORIGINAL 2003 EDITION ONLY

Not everything that was in the 2003 edition of the DVD is found in the 2013 edition, and several sections from the original have vanished. These are the following:
a) Controls: - Classic Pass; - Hindu Shuffle Pass; - One Handed Top Palm; - Elmsley Count
b) Forces: - Riffle Force

CONTENT ON THE NEW 2003 EDITION ONLY

The new edition is about twice as long, and that's because some of the explanations are more detailed and elaborate, but also because it has some new material. Here's a list of all the new topics:
a) Controls: - Gamblers Cop; - False Overhand Shuffle
b) Forces: - Dribble Force; - Spread Cull Force
c) Flourishes: - Swivel Change
d) Routines: - Slop Shuffle; - Card to Mouth; - Blossom Production

COMPARISON

It's a pity to see some material from the original version left out in the new version, especially the Riffle Force (given its popularity), and the Elmsley Count (given that it is used in so many tricks). I'm also curious why a decision was made to eliminate the Pass, although this is a more advanced move that is typically beyond the scope of beginners, and perhaps that's why it was removed. If material was going to be added, the Glide would have been an obvious addition as a fairly basic sleight, so I'm surprised that this continues to be an omission from what is intended to be a good introduction to fundamentals.

But the additional elements are very solid, and the increased number of routines in the new version will especially be welcome to many people. What I like most, however, is the fact that the sleights and moves are taught in a little more detail, and I think the more elaborate explanations here are very helpful.

Oz himself appears to have lost some of his youthful innocence, and comes across a little more brash and almost over-confident. I'm still not quite sure what to make of his new business suit attired persona, or whether I prefer the younger Oz. But he is still an excellent teacher, and that's really what this video is all about. It's always been an excellent choice for relative beginners looking to get a good handle on the fundamentals of card magic, and overall this enhanced version only makes a good thing even better.

RECOMMENDATION

Oz Pearlman's "Born to Perform Card Magic" DVD has always been applauded for being a great introduction to the fundamentals of card magic, and one of the better entries in this genre. The updated and expanded 2013 edition takes something that is a proven success, and expands it for a new audience that may not be familiar with the original. It still deserves to be one of the top recommendations for new magicians, and continues to be an excellent starting point to give beginners a good head-start with their card magic.

- BoardGameGeek reviewer EndersGame
